key_growth_drivers: Generative AI and ML workload surge driving massive cloud infrastructure investment (GPU-accelerated compute, AI-as-a-Service)
Digital transformation acceleration — ~60% of enterprises require major IT/cloud modernization (IDC 2024)
Hybrid and multi-cloud strategy adoption — Gartner predicts 90% of orgs will adopt hybrid cloud by 2027
Expansion of SaaS, IaaS, and PaaS consumption across BFSI, healthcare, manufacturing, and government sectors
Remote/hybrid work proliferation sustaining demand for cloud collaboration, storage, and productivity tools
key_headwinds: Cloud cost management complexity — managing spend is the #1 limitation to growth (Flexera)
Data sovereignty, localization regulations, and cross-border data governance (GDPR, national frameworks)
Cybersecurity and data privacy concerns increasing compliance burden for enterprises
Capacity constraints and power/energy availability limiting data center build-out for AI workloads
Skills gap — shortage of cloud-native and AI-cloud expertise slowing enterprise adoption
confidence_note: Sources diverge significantly on TAM depending on market scope definition. Gartner's $723B (2025) covers public cloud end-user spending only. MarketsandMarkets' $1.29T includes broader IaaS/PaaS/SaaS. Mordor Intelligence ($860B) and Precedence Research ($912B) represent mid-range estimates. All sources agree on strong double-digit CAGR driven by AI, but CAGRs range from 12%–25.6% depending on scope and methodology. Confidence rated 'medium' due to this divergence.
